seed_rule_set.description: Generic forecast trajectory rubric. It evaluates whether a ReAct forecasting agent understands a resolvable event, retrieves reliable evidence, reasons faithfully, updates probabilities, and uses tools efficiently.

dimensions.name: Question understanding and resolvability
dimensions.description: Whether the agent identifies event subject, time, place, conditions, and settlement criteria.
dimensions.score_5: Clearly extracts event subject, deadline, options, settlement rule, ambiguity, and edge cases.
dimensions.score_3: Understands the broad event but misses some boundary or settlement details.
dimensions.score_1: Treats an open-ended or non-resolvable prompt as directly forecastable.

dimensions.name: Information retrieval quality
dimensions.description: Whether retrieval covers multiple relevant, independent, fresh, and documented sources.
dimensions.score_5: Uses multiple independent, relevant sources and records search/fetch paths.
dimensions.score_3: Uses relevant sources but lacks diversity, freshness, or trace detail.
dimensions.score_1: Uses sparse, irrelevant, duplicate, or undocumented retrieval.

dimensions.name: Source credibility and news quality
dimensions.description: Whether evidence comes from authoritative, transparent, accurate, and independent sources.
dimensions.score_5: Prioritizes primary/official/high-quality sources and distinguishes source independence.
dimensions.score_3: Uses generally credible secondary sources but limited primary verification.
dimensions.score_1: Relies on low-quality, unclear, promotional, or duplicated sources.

dimensions.name: Reasoning chain quality
dimensions.description: Whether the agent links evidence to forecast-relevant variables and uncertainty.
dimensions.score_5: Separates facts, assumptions, counter-evidence, causal links, and uncertainty clearly.
dimensions.score_3: Provides plausible reasoning but weak conflict handling or causal explanation.
dimensions.score_1: Stacks news snippets without auditable evidence-to-forecast rationale.

dimensions.name: Probability update quality
dimensions.description: Whether probabilities are updated from evidence with calibrated magnitude and residual uncertainty.
dimensions.score_5: Shows evidence-driven probability movement, reasonable magnitude, and remaining uncertainty.
dimensions.score_3: Probability is connected to evidence but update magnitude is underspecified.
dimensions.score_1: Probability appears arbitrary or jumps to extremes from weak evidence.

dimensions.name: Tool use and execution efficiency
dimensions.description: Whether the agent uses necessary tools without wasteful or irrelevant steps.
dimensions.score_5: Uses appropriate search/fetch/reasoning steps with complete ReAct trajectories and controlled cost.
dimensions.score_3: Uses the right tools but has some redundant or shallow steps.
dimensions.score_1: Misses necessary tools or performs mostly irrelevant tool calls.

dimensions.name: Settlement rule extraction
dimensions.description: Evaluate settlement rule extraction for sports forecast trajectories using only trajectory, evidence, and report artifacts.
dimensions.score_5: Excellent settlement rule extraction with explicit, source-backed, cutoff-compliant support.
dimensions.score_3: Partial settlement rule extraction with some gaps or weak traceability.
dimensions.score_1: Missing or unreliable settlement rule extraction.

rounds.feedback: round_1_candidate_1 is currently best. Validation score=0.0, corr(resolved probability)=0.0, corr(-Brier)=0.0, corr(accuracy)=0.0. Improve dimensions that better distinguish credible evidence, cutoff compliance, and probability calibration.

dimension_scores.rationale: The trajectory has a complete ReAct record and explicit probability output.
Retrieval is sparse or low-diversity.
Sources are mostly synthetic, missing, or unverifiable.
The trajectory combines direct evidence, opposing evidence, and complete ReAct reasoning.
The probability output is supported by enough cutoff-compliant evidence.
Expected sub-agents and tool actions are complete.
Generic dimension lacks enough matching evidence.
